seed.goal: Your agent receives a short internal instruction from the manager: Cancel all Pine Tree project meetings and notify the Pine Tree team that the project has been shelved due to global warming concerns.The agent must interpret this directive, gather context from email history, identify the correct set of recipients, and execute two distinct email-based tasks.
seed.synthetic_domain:
Cancel all Pine Tree project meetings and notify the Pine Tree team that the project has been shelved due to global warming concerns.The agent must interpret this directive, gather context from email history, identify the correct set of recipients, and execute two distinct email-based tasks.

Expanded scenario and context (for synthetic data generation and downstream evaluation):

A. Scenario framing

You are an AI operations assistant embedded within the Environmental Initiatives Office at Northwind Traders, a global retail distribution company that manages major supply chains and partners with numerous outdoor brands. The company has an internal cross-functional project called “Pine Tree,” focused on developing sustainable packaging aligned with Northwind Traders’ green commitments. Leadership has recently decided to shelve the Pine Tree project due to concerns that the underlying business model cannot adapt to stricter global warming regulations anticipated next quarter. The agent’s core responsibility is to act on a short, unambiguous directive from a manager: cancel all scheduled Pine Tree project meetings (immediately and going forward) and email the Pine Tree project team to formally notify them of the project’s suspension, citing global warming concerns. The agent must carry this out efficiently and with sensitivity, as the project has high visibility internally and involves contributors from multiple teams.

B. High-level task:
- Parse the manager’s internal email/directive to extract the cancellation and notification requirements.
- Gather historical context from previous email threads and distribution lists to identify the full set of Pine Tree project participants.
- Locate and cancel all meetings (recurring and one-off) related to the Pine Tree project in the company’s centralized calendar system.
- Draft and send a clear, sensitive email notification to the entire Pine Tree project team, explaining the project cessation and its cause.
- Optionally, notify meeting invitees about the cancellations if not covered by the main project notification email.
- Ensure all relevant stakeholders are updated, avoiding duplicate/conflicting communication.
- Success is judged by thorough cancellation (no project meetings remain); all stakeholders are appropriately and diplomatically notified with no one left uninformed or blindsided.

C. Organizations and environment:
- Primary organization: Northwind Traders
  - Industry: Global supply chain and retail distribution; 2,500+ employees across North America, Europe, and APAC (main time zones: America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Singapore).
  - Primary products: Retail distribution logistics, sustainable packaging R&D, supply chain consulting.
  - Markets: Worldwide logistics and retail.
- No direct external organization involved for this scenario (purely internal); Pine Tree project is cross-team within Northwind Traders.
- Business context:
  - Northwind Traders launched Pine Tree six months ago, investing significant resources. However, sudden regulatory shifts regarding global warming have forced suspension—potential job impacts, morale shifts, and resource reallocation at stake. Notifying staff sensitively is paramount to maintain trust.
- Core systems involved:
  - Corporate email system.
  - Centralized calendar/meeting scheduling platform.
  - Internal distribution lists, project channel archives, and document repository for Pine Tree context.

D. Personas and stakeholders:
- Andre Lawson (andre.lawson@northwindtraders.com)
  - Sustainability Program Manager, Environmental Initiatives (Primary, America/New_York)
  - Values clarity and empathy; prefers direct communication with rich context.
- Amari Rivera (amari.rivera@northwindtraders.com)
  - Head of Strategy, Pine Tree Project (Primary, Europe/Berlin)
  - Early riser, prefers early-morning meetings, values comprehensive overviews.
- Hailey Clark (hailey.clark@northwindtraders.com)
  - Senior Research Analyst, R&D (Secondary, Asia/Singapore)
  - Often traveling; prefers asynchronous, concise updates.
- Jamie Evans (jamie.evans@northwindtraders.com)
  - Operations Supervisor, Distribution (Secondary, Europe/Berlin)
  - Respects formal protocols, dislikes informal/sloppy emails.
- Wesley Brooks (wesley.brooks@northwindtraders.com)
  - Executive Assistant, Environmental Initiatives (Optional, America/New_York)
  - Follows up on calendar logistics and administrivia.
- Parker McLean (parker.mclean@northwindtraders.com)
  - Junior Sustainability Analyst, Pine Tree Team (Optional, America/New_York)
  - New to company, unsure of project distribution list conventions.
- Christoper Reed (christoper.reed@northwindtraders.com)
  - Manager, HR Business Partner (Optional, America/New_York)
  - May need to support impacted staff; values early heads up.
- Eugenia Lopez (eugenia.lopez@northwindtraders.com)
  - IT Systems Admin (Optional, Europe/Berlin)
  - Supports calendaring/email group management; rarely involved unless there is a delivery failure or bounced message.

E. Data sources and artifacts for synthetic data:
- Internal directive email: single, succinct instruction from the manager (less than 100 words), referencing "Pine Tree project" and global warming.
- Historical Pine Tree project email threads (formal subject lines, distributed to "Pine Tree Team" group alias and direct members); sometimes ambiguous participant lists as people come and go.
- Meeting invitations: calendar objects with title, agenda, description, recurrence, participant list, and attached docs/links; some meetings recurring, some one-off.
- Project distribution list records: access-controlled CSV/roster files sometimes referenced in emails; not always up-to-date—people sometimes missed or erroneously included.
- Notification email(s): drafts for communicating cancellation; must balance formality and empathy; may require translation for APAC/EMEA if relevant.

F. Operational constraints and requirements:
- Business rules:
  - Only Pine Tree-related meetings to be cancelled; do not touch unrelated meetings.
  - Notify all who are currently or recently active; consult last 60 days of project threads to catch recent additions.
- Time constraints:
  - Notification must happen today; all meetings must be cancelled with at least 24 hours’ notice if possible.
  - Follow office hours for each region; do not send middle-of-the-night notifications.
- Quality constraints:
  - Tone should be professional, empathetic, and clear—avoid blame. Use inclusive language about next steps.
  - Ensure no recipients outside Pine Tree project are included.
  - Project distribution list must be updated if obvious out-of-date members are detected.
- Compliance:
  - Do not include sensitive personnel details in emails.
- Tie-breaking/prioritization:
  - If recipient ambiguity, err on notifying more people, not less (within internal confidentiality bounds).
  - If cancellation/notification might overlap, combine into a single message to minimize inbox clutter.

G. Edge and negative cases to include in synthetic data:
- Meetings without “Pine Tree” in the title but clearly about Pine Tree (based on agenda or invite list).
- Overlapping or duplicate calendar invites sent to different subsets of team members.
- Former team members still present on distribution lists, or missing recent joiners.
- Stakeholders out of office (auto-replies), with whom follow-up is required.
- Meetings already cancelled previously but still showing as recurring on some attendees’ calendars.
- Recipients who reply-all asking for clarification, clarification requests about next steps or alternative assignments.
- Cross-timezone confusion (recipients confused about meeting local times, or getting notification at odd local hours).
- Distribution list aliases that bounce or return "unknown recipient" errors.
- A stakeholder objects to the “global warming” rationale and requests a more technical explanation.
- Ambiguous requests to “forward to the broader Sustainability group” without list details.
- Internal note referencing regulatory documents or leadership decisions not attached or linked properly.
- Notification email draft is accidentally sent to the entire company (must not occur).

H. Input characteristics for synthetic data:
- Variability in tone: Some emails are formal and template-driven, others are informal (e.g., colleagues using "Hey team" openings).
- Occasional typos, corporate acronyms, and jargon (“PT Q4 standup”, “EIO sync”, “DL-PineTree”).
- Some messages concise and to-the-point, others lengthy with background.
- Occasional greetings/closings in German (“Guten Morgen”, “Mit freundlichen Grüßen”) or Mandarin (“谢谢”, “祝好”).
- Sample meeting invite subject lines:
  - “Pine Tree Weekly Stakeholder Sync”
  - “URGENT: Pine Tree—Packaging Sustainability Review”
  - “Pine Tree: Research & Compliance Sprint”
  - “EIO | Pine Tree Project Check-in”
  - “Q2 Pine Tree Wrap-Up – All Hands”
- Template for meetings: title includes “Pine Tree”, but sometimes descriptive-only (e.g., “Sustainability Packaging Review” refers to Pine Tree in body).
- Occasional violations: invites without clear agendas, ad-hoc calendar invites from different assistants.

I. Expected outputs (conceptual, for evaluation and synthetic data alignment):
- Structured outputs:
  - List of cancelled meetings (title, datetime, recurrence, affected attendees, calendar ID/reference).
  - Notification email object: recipients (distribution list/addressed individuals), subject, body, send timestamps, delivery status.
  - Update logs: record of actions (for audit), including relevant email/cancellation traces.
- Human-readable:
  - Email notifications (clear subject lines, paragraph explanation, optional bullet summary of next steps/channels for questions).
  - Individual meeting cancellation notices if not combined in the notification.
  - Tone: neutral to positive, constructive, offering contacts for further discussion.
- Outputs should be consistent: emails sent to those whose meetings are cancelled, content reflects meeting/action status accurately, no mismatches.

J. Behavioral expectations (for downstream evaluation, not tool prescription):
- Correctly identify all Pine Tree-related meetings and relevant team members, even with ambiguous naming/aliasing.
- Gather and reconcile attendee information from emails, meeting invites, and recent context within the last 60 days.
- Cancel all relevant meetings promptly, prioritizing those happening soonest.
- Draft and send empathetic, professional notifications explaining the decision; avoid insensitive or abrupt language.
- Respect working-hour windows by time zone for notifications and cancellations.
- Alert (not silently skip) if the distribution list appears out of date or a cancellation fails to deliver.
- Avoid cancelling unrelated meetings or over-notifying those not involved.
- Absorb and integrate context from multiple noisy sources, resolving discrepancies sensibly.
- When uncertain about attendance or aliasing, opt for inclusive notification consistent with privacy constraints.
- Do not hallucinate causes; use only facts in the directive plus supplied rationale.
- Escalate or annotate ambiguous or risky cases (e.g., suspected data drift in recipient list).

K. Content guidelines for synthetic data:
- CRITICAL: Use ONLY company names and domains from the provided fictional companies list.
  - All companies, distribution lists, and email addresses must use the “northwindtraders.com” domain.
  - Do NOT invent new company names or domains.
- Use realistic corporate email formats (firstname.lastname@northwindtraders.com).
- All personas, organizations, emails, and time zones should be internally consistent with this scenario (Northwind Traders, Pine Tree project, global time zones).
- Use contextually plausible numbers, project dates, and regulatory details.
- Mix formal and semi-casual English styles, with occasional minor typos or informal language in example artifacts.
- Avoid real-world tool/naming references—generic “company calendar system”, “email”, “project channel”.
- Ensure enough messiness (typos, missing data, ambiguous aliases, cross-timezone complexity) to enable robust synthetic data and evaluation.

Dear Operations Team,

Effective immediately, please cancel all upcoming and scheduled meetings related to the Pine Tree project. This action is required due to new global warming regulations and significant environmental concerns impacting our business model. Ensure the entire project team is notified today of the project's suspension and the rationale behind this decision.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this important matter.

Best regards,
Emma Clark
Environmental Initiatives Office

tc_tc-collaboration-email-1_mock_1.subject: Pine Tree Project – Immediate Suspension of Activities
tc_tc-collaboration-email-1_mock_1.body_md:
Hallo Pine Tree Team,

I want to inform you that, following yesterday’s leadership review and in response to significant regulatory changes and the evolving global warming situation, the Pine Tree project is being suspended effective immediately.

**Key points:**
- All Pine Tree project meetings—recurring and one-offs—are now canceled.
- No further project activities should be conducted until further notice.
- This decision does *not* reflect on the team's outstanding commitment and professionalism; the circumstances are unfortunately beyond our control.

**Background:**
The Northwind Traders Environmental Initiatives Office has continuously monitored the latest climate science and compliance needs. Given the new legal frameworks in our core markets (Europe/Berlin, Asia/Singapore, US East), it is no longer feasible to continue under the current operational model. Global warming risk factors have accelerated sooner than projections.

**Next steps:**
- Please stand down on all in-progress work and notify any external partners accordingly (cc comms if unclear).
- If you have unaddressed deliverables, park these for now—do *not* delete or archive files until further guidance is given.
- Any questions or feedback, reach out directly to myself or Amari Rivera (cc’d).

**Danke und谢谢大家 for your dedication over the last quarters!**

Let’s discuss next week in a short team huddle (invite to follow for those affected by additional transitions). For those who joined Pine Tree recently or just wrapped up previous deliverables: thank you again – your contributions are recognized and will inform upcoming Northwind projects.

Best regards,

Andre Lawson  
Manager, Environmental Initiatives Office  
Northwind Traders

tc_tc-collaboration-email-2_mock_1.subject: URGENT: Pine Tree Project Suspension & Meeting Cancellations
tc_tc-collaboration-email-2_mock_1.body_md:
Hey team,

I’m writing with some difficult news. Due to the latest global warming regulations and executive guidance, the **Pine Tree project is being suspended effective immediately**. All associated meetings, standups, and syncs over the next month (including "PT Q4 Stakeholder Sync," "DL-PineTree Connect," and any meetings referenced in recent agendas) are hereby canceled. Please ensure you update your calendars to avoid confusion.

For clarity:
- **All Pine Tree meetings scheduled after today are canceled.**
- If you received invites under aliases like "DL-PineTree" or see your name on attached agendas (see agenda.docx), assume you’re included—err on the side of attending brief check-ins ONLY if explicitly prompted. 
- Distribution lists will be reconciled by EIO to reflect roster changes. If you notice wrong recipients (recent leavers/joiners), reply to update. 

### Next steps
- **No further Pine Tree work should proceed until future notice**. If you’re unsure about project boundaries (some workstreams may overlap with "Packaging Sustainability," "PT Regulatory," etc.), reply for clarification or check with EIO.
- All hands originally planned for next Friday is canceled.
- If you have documentation or open questions, send to environmental.office@northwindtraders.com for archiving.

_Guten Morgen_, and apologies for abrupt change—regulatory landscape is evolving fast. Let’s stay connected and support each other through the transition.

Best,
Angela
Environmental Initiatives Office

**Attachment referenced:** agenda.docx

test_cases.description: Test the agent's ability to identify all recurring Pine Tree project meetings that should be cancelled while recognizing that certain one-time sessions (like final budget reviews or closeout meetings) should remain scheduled despite the project suspension. The agent must distinguish between recurring series and standalone events, handle overlapping time slots, and communicate the selective identification strategy clearly.
test_cases.input: I need you to check my calendar for the next 60 days and identify all recurring Pine Tree project meetings that need to be cancelled immediately - the project has been suspended. However, any one-time meetings that are specifically about project closeout, final budget reviews, or wrap-up activities should be kept since we still need those to properly wind down the project. Can you review what's scheduled and let me know which meetings should be cancelled versus which ones should be kept?

arguments.name: filter
assertion: Argument 'filter' contains search criteria for 'Pine Tree' related calendar events

arguments.name: startDateTime
assertion: Argument 'startDateTime' is provided and represents current or near-current date

arguments.name: endDateTime
assertion: Argument 'endDateTime' is provided and represents approximately 60 days from startDateTime as specified in the user request

test_cases.expected_response:
I've reviewed your Pine Tree project calendar for the next 60 days. Here's what I found:

**Meetings to Cancel (Recurring Series):**
- Pine Tree Weekly Stakeholder Sync (recurring every Friday at 9:00 AM)
- Pine Tree EMEA Regional Sync (recurring every Monday at 3:00 PM)
- Pine Tree Q4 Development Standup (recurring every Thursday at 10:00 AM)

test_cases.name: Identify Ambiguously-Named Pine Tree Meetings to Cancel Through Context Analysis
test_cases.description: Test the agent's ability to identify Pine Tree project meetings that don't explicitly contain 'Pine Tree' in the title, requiring analysis of meeting descriptions, attendees, and email context to determine project affiliation. The agent must handle ambiguous meeting names like 'Weekly Project Operations Sync' or 'Environmental Policy Review' that only mention Pine Tree in descriptions or agendas, cross-reference with email threads for context, and make reasoned decisions about which meetings should be cancelled while documenting their reasoning for ambiguous cases.
test_cases.input: I need you to identify all Pine Tree project meetings for the next 90 days that should be cancelled. Be thorough - some meetings don't have 'Pine Tree' in the title but are definitely Pine Tree related based on the agenda or who's attending. Check my recent emails and meeting descriptions to figure out which ones are actually for Pine Tree, and let me know which meetings should be cancelled and why you determined they were Pine Tree-related, especially for the less obvious ones.

arguments.name: queryString
assertion: SearchMessages 'queryString' contains terms related to 'Pine Tree' and/or meeting-related keywords to gather context

arguments.name: size
assertion: SearchMessages 'size' argument is set to a reasonable value (50-100) for comprehensive context gathering

arguments.name: filter
assertion: Argument 'filter' shows the agent is searching for calendar events that may be Pine Tree-related

arguments.name: startDateTime
assertion: Argument 'startDateTime' is provided and represents current or near-current date

arguments.name: endDateTime
assertion: Argument 'endDateTime' is provided and represents approximately 90 days from startDateTime

test_cases.expected_response:
I've completed a thorough search for Pine Tree project meetings over the next 90 days. Through email context analysis and calendar review, I identified the following meetings that should be cancelled:

**Explicitly Named:**
- Pine Tree Project Kickoff (1/15)
- Pine Tree Weekly Status Update (recurring, all instances)

**Identified Through Context Analysis:**
- 'Weekly Project Operations Sync' - meeting description references Pine Tree environmental compliance initiatives and key Pine Tree stakeholders are attendees
- 'Q1 Environmental Policy Review' - email threads confirm this meeting focuses on Pine Tree regulatory requirements

These meetings should be cancelled and a notification should be sent to stakeholders explaining the cancellations and the reasoning for including the less obvious meetings.

response_quality_expectation.assertion: The agent demonstrates analytical reasoning by identifying Pine Tree meetings including explicitly named ones like 'Pine Tree Project Kickoff' and 'Pine Tree Weekly Status Update', as well as ambiguously named ones like 'Weekly Project Operations Sync', 'Q1 Environmental Policy Review', and 'Sustainability Packaging Workshop' through contextual clues (descriptions showing they involve hailey.clark@northwindtraders.com, priya.desai@northwindtraders.com, quincy.brooks@northwindtraders.com, or mention Pine Tree in meeting descriptions). The agent explains the reasoning for ambiguous classifications and provides a comprehensive list of meetings that should be cancelled with detailed explanation.

tc_4d48ce3dd95d49f1_mock_0.subject: Need comprehensive Pine Tree meeting cancellation - including less obvious ones
tc_4d48ce3dd95d49f1_mock_0.body_md:
Hi,

I need you to find and cancel ALL meetings related to the Pine Tree environmental initiative project for the next 90 days. This includes not just meetings with 'Pine Tree' explicitly in the title, but also any meetings that are actually about Pine Tree even if they use different naming.

Some context to help: Pine Tree is our sustainability/environmental compliance project, so meetings discussing environmental policy, regulatory compliance, or sustainability packaging that involve our Pine Tree team members are likely related. Also check meeting descriptions and attendee lists - if you see hailey.clark@northwindtraders.com, priya.desai@northwindtraders.com, or quincy.brooks@northwindtraders.com (our core Pine Tree team), that's a strong indicator.

Please be thorough in your search and explain your reasoning for the less obvious ones. Once you've identified them all, cancel them and send a summary to the Pine Tree distribution list.

tc_4d48ce3dd95d49f1_mock_1.subject: Pine Tree Team: Upcoming meetings and context
tc_4d48ce3dd95d49f1_mock_1.body_md:
Hi everyone,

Just a quick update on our upcoming meetings:

1. **Weekly Project Operations Sync** (Fridays 10am) - This is where we discuss Pine Tree environmental compliance status, vendor relationships, and implementation roadblocks. Julia and Naomi lead this.

2. **Q1 Environmental Policy Review** (Thursday 2pm) - Regulatory team meeting focused on Pine Tree's compliance with new environmental regulations. Critical for keeping the project on track.

3. **Sustainability Packaging Workshop** (Recurring Tuesdays) - Working session on Pine Tree's packaging strategy to meet environmental goals.

All of these are essential to Pine Tree progress even though they don't have 'Pine Tree' in the calendar title. Please make sure you're attending the ones relevant to your role.

Best,
Hailey Clark
Senior Research Analyst

test_cases.name: Identify Imminent Pine Tree Meetings to Cancel and Send Urgent Team Notification
test_cases.description: Test the agent's ability to prioritize time-sensitive actions by identifying Pine Tree meetings happening within the next 48 hours that need to be cancelled immediately, and sending an urgent notification to all affected participants explaining the last-minute cancellation. The agent must demonstrate understanding of urgency, extract the right attendee list, and compose an appropriate apologetic message for short-notice cancellations.
test_cases.input: This is urgent - I just found out the Pine Tree project is being suspended effective immediately due to regulatory issues. Can you check my calendar for any Pine Tree meetings in the next 48 hours that need to be cancelled right away? Then please send an email to everyone who was supposed to attend those meetings explaining the situation and apologizing for the extremely short notice. Make sure to mention this is due to unforeseen regulatory compliance requirements.

arguments.name: filter
assertion: Argument 'filter' contains search criteria for 'Pine Tree' related calendar events

arguments.name: startDateTime
assertion: Argument 'startDateTime' is provided and represents current date/time for immediate meeting check

arguments.name: endDateTime
assertion: Argument 'endDateTime' is provided and represents approximately 48 hours from startDateTime as specified in user request

arguments.name: to
assertion: sendMail 'to' argument contains email addresses of attendees from the cancelled 48-hour meetings, specifically andre.lawson@northwindtraders.com, phoenix.ray@northwindtraders.com, riley.chen@northwindtraders.com, michael.peltier@northwindtraders.com, and juan.morgan@northwindtraders.com

arguments.name: subject
assertion: sendMail 'subject' indicates urgency and mentions Pine Tree meeting cancellations
sendMail 'subject' is between 10-200 characters

arguments.name: body
assertion: sendMail 'body' includes apology for short notice
sendMail 'body' mentions regulatory compliance requirements as the reason
sendMail 'body' lists the specific meetings that need to be cancelled with dates/times, such as 'Pine Tree Status Update', 'Pine Tree Team Standup', and 'Pine Tree Stakeholder Sync'

test_cases.expected_response:

2. Pine Tree Team Standup - Tomorrow (June 7) at 9:30 AM

**Notification Sent To:**

The email explained the immediate suspension due to regulatory compliance requirements and apologized for the extremely short notice. All affected team members have been notified.

tc_urgent_cancel_mock_1.subject: URGENT: Pine Tree Meetings Cancelled - Regulatory Compliance Issue
tc_urgent_cancel_mock_1.body_md:
Dear Pine Tree Team,

I apologize for the extremely short notice, but I must inform you that the following Pine Tree meetings scheduled for the next 48 hours have been cancelled effective immediately:

- **Pine Tree Team Standup** - Tomorrow (June 7) at 9:30 AM
- **Pine Tree Status Update** - Tomorrow (June 7) at 2:00 PM  
- **Pine Tree Stakeholder Sync** - June 8 at 11:00 AM

This sudden cancellation is due to **unforeseen regulatory compliance requirements** that require us to suspend all Pine Tree project activities immediately while we address these matters.

I understand this is very disruptive given the short timeline, and I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience. We will provide updates on the project status as soon as we have more information.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards
